channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Trump urges ICE to keep using traffic stops after deadly incidents
item.description: US president backs tactic despite agency announcing temporary pause after fatal shootings in Texas and Maine.

item.title: Who is calling the shots in Iran amid the deadly war with the US?
item.description: The IRGC has taken the lead in Iran, particularly over Hormuz - and the government says it's on the same page.

item.title: England vs Argentina LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026 semifinal
item.description: Live coverage and text updates as England and Argentina renew one of football's great rivalries in the semifinal.

item.title: July 15, 10 years on: Turkiye’s will, Turkiye’s victory
item.description: The defeat of the 2016 coup attempt helped usher in a decade of greater diplomatic, economic and military power.

item.title: How US-Iran escalation will test Iraq’s balancing act
item.description: The Iraqi PM visited the White House to deepen economic ties, as Iraq-based allies of Iran threatened to fight the US.

item.title: My Twitter, not X
item.description: As Twitter, then X, marks 20 years since its launch, a journalist journeys through revolutions and wars.

item.title: Legal challenge says Trump ICC sanctions tread on US citizens’ rights
item.description: Groups charge that sanctions against ICC, Palestinian rights organisations stifle freedom of speech and expression.

item.title: Iraq’s prime minister carries the title, but not the power
item.description: Al-Zaidi’s Washington visit exposed the pressure reshaping Baghdad’s politics, security and economy.

item.title: What DOGE saved, and what it cost
item.description: The US government project that slashed foreign aid died on July 4. AJLabs looks at the cost in lives around the world.

item.title: World Cup final Lamine Yamal: What to know about teenage Spain prodigy
item.description: One of the best players in the world, Spain's 19-year-old Lamine Yamal, is gearing up for his first World Cup final.

item.title: Far-right Israeli lawmaker raids Palestinian school and threatens shutdown
item.description: Israel’s education committee head stormed a school in Jerusalem, vowing to shut it down over Palestinian symbols.

item.title: Protest in Pakistan’s Kashmir stalls after nine killed in clashes
item.description: At least 28 people have been killed since protests began in June, as the region braces for a major protest march.

item.title: Spain’s Oscar-winning actor raises Palestine flag at World Cup match
item.description: Spain's Oscar-winning actor Javier Bardem had a message for Palestinians as he raised Palestine flag during World Cup.

item.title: US strikes marine control tower in Iran
item.description: US strikes marine control tower in Iran

item.title: As the US restarts war on Iran, is its weapons stockpile running low?
item.description: The US has expended a significant number of munitions since the war began in February.

item.title: Utah man arrested after allegedly targeting Muslim kiosk worker in stabbing
item.description: Rights groups condemn the attack and warn against recent anti-immigration rhetoric.

item.title: Will Lindsey Graham’s death affect Israel’s standing in Washington?
item.description: It might in the short term, but not in the long term.

item.title: Israeli air attack on Gaza apartment kills family of three
item.description: One child survives hit on Deir el-Balah home as Israel escalates attacks across territory despite October 'ceasefire'.

item.title: DRC Ebola cases surpass 2,000 as more health workers begin strike
item.description: Health workers in Democratic Republic of the Congo's Ituri province strike over unpaid wages as death toll rises to 754.

item.title: India-UK trade deal comes into effect: What’s cheaper in each country now?
item.description: The deal cuts tariffs on thousands of goods and widens access for services firms and ​professionals in both markets.

item.title: Why has Lindsey Graham’s sister inherited his Senate seat after his death?
item.description: The late senator's sister, the politically inexperienced Darline Graham Nordone, has inherited his office and powers.

item.title: ‘Cycle of chaos’: Israel killing Gaza civil officials to derail its future
item.description: Israel's relentless Gaza attacks aim to sabotage the 'ceasefire' and ensure perpetual chaos, analysts warn.

item.title: Could the US take control of Iran’s southern islands?
item.description: Operation would be a dramatic military spectacle but would carry costs analysts doubt Washington is willing to incur.

item.title: What are the Israeli withdrawal ‘pilot zones’ in southern Lebanon?
item.description: What are the Israeli withdrawal ‘pilot zones’ in southern Lebanon?

item.title: Cody Bellinger stars at MLB All-Star game, wins MVP award
item.description: With his father - former big leaguer Clay Bellinger - in attendance, the New York Yankees player earned MVP honours.
